description: Lock electrochemical_industry to the 1800 Voltaic-electrolysis milestone rather than later industrial electrochemical production.

checks.reason: Nicholson, Carlisle, and Cruickshank published their Voltaic decomposition experiments in 1800.
Continuous electrical current is the constitutive electrolysis mechanism.
The scoped process cannot operate without electric current.
Chemical identification of gases, solutions, and decomposition products remains a direct enabling context.
Chemical knowledge enabled interpretation and extension of the experiments without defining the electrical mechanism.
Early Voltaic electrochemistry remains upstream of the graph's early electroplating milestone.
